project: Arlington will pass through funds to Housing Corporation of Arlington (HCA) to acquire parcel adjacent to existing project, creating additional 30 new low-income units in 3A district.

The Cities of Malden and Revere are partnering to advance a regional transportation improvement project that will unlock 737 units of mixed-income housing units, 112 key hotel and 14,000 sf of retail as the final phase of development at Overlook Ridge. The two-communities have combined their permitting and approval process to advance this $460M development phase to create 219 affordable units, 1,400 construction jobs and 140 permanent jobs for the region.
Medfield will build the infrastructure required to unlock a $235M redevelopment of Medfield State Hospital into a 334-unit mixed income residential development with 25% affordable units.
Newburyport will conduct site preparation activities, including demolition, to support the redevelopment of the Brown School into 29 deeply affordable senior housing units.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: Revere will pass through funds to The Neighborhood Developers (TND) to acquire a parcel to build up to 300 new units.

Easton will develop a climate and energy master plan taking into consideration climate-related efforts undertaken by the Town to date, identifying major priorities and opportunities to achieve additional energy efficiencies, reduce carbon emission, and increase resiliency.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: Easton will develop a climate and energy master plan taking into consideration climate-related efforts undertaken by the Town to date, identifying major priorities and opportunities to achieve additional energy efficiencies, reduce carbon emission, and increase resiliency.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: The Gloucester Water Pollution Control Facility (WPCF) Improvements Project will upgrade treatment performance to meet secondary treatment standards at the WPCF’s current rated capacity as well as addressing aging assets and new solids management facilities. Secondary treatment will allow the City to meet the requirements of the current United States Environmental Protection Agency (USEPA) Consent Decree and its new National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) Discharge Permit.
Malden will transform one third of Malden's Department of Public Works Yard into a climate resilient waterfront park in an environmental justice neighborhood.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: Revere will design a new drainage system to improve stormwater management in the Riverside Neighborhood is located on the Pines River, a documented EJ population vulnerable to climate change, excessive pollution, and an outdated undersized municipal drainage system.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: This project will construct safety and multimodal improvements along the public ROW on Mass Avenue between Richardson Ave and Quinn Rd in Arlington. The project will accommodate new residential and business development in the immediate area, including a planned boutique hotel/restaurant and the newly constructed 124-unit mixed affordable and market rate housing development.
Chelsea will conduct site preparation activities to leverage mixed-use development of 100 units (15% affordable) and 7 retail spaces at 115 Park Street.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: This project is Phase III of the King Street Commons Public Infrastructure Project at 550 King Street, a development with 804 housing units, 134,000 square feet of commercial space, and 480,000 square feet of office space. Construction will be done to improve 1,300 feet of roadway, sewer, water, stormwater, traffic, lighting, landscaping/streetscape and bicycle/pedestrian access. The project will create 2,600 construction jobs and 1,180 permanent full-time jobs.

Marlborough will complete phase 3 of the Lincoln Street Reconstruction Project and make targeted improvements along Lincoln Street from Highland Street to Bolton Street to unlock new residential growth and private investment in this EJ area.

This award provides funding for traffic improvements to support the first phase of the transformative 161-acre Suffolk Downs redevelopment project to include 51,000 sf of life science space, 948 new residential units, 71,500 sf of retail, and a 159-key hotel. Four targeted traffic improvements funded by this grant will unlock $632M in private investment to catalyze the regionally significant transit-oriented mixed-use redevelopment project.

This project will make safety and multimodal improvements along the public right-of-way on Main Street (Route 28) corridor. Funding will be used to develop the final roadway design which will accommodate the redevelopment of a large parcel into a dense, mixed use project.

Upton will extend water and sewer infrastructure east from Upton Town Center along Route 140 and directly support a new private development with residences for adults 55+, townhouses, and a mixed-use component as well as unlock the development potential.

Funding supports infrastructure repairs, upgrades and enhancements to support a $175 million redevelopment in downtown Haverhill that will transform the failed Urban Renewal District and Goecke Municipal Parking Deck into a vibrant public plaza and mixed-use development. This public infrastructure project creates the new public plaza, roadway, sidewalk and streetscape improvements on the surrounding street network.

Funding supports infrastructure repairs, upgrades and enhancements to support a $175 million redevelopment in downtown Haverhill that will transform the failed Urban Renewal District and Goecke Municipal Parking Deck into a vibrant public plaza and mixed-use development. This public infrastructure project creates the new public plaza, roadway, sidewalk and streetscape improvements on the surrounding street network.
Littleton will design and construct public infrastructure to leverage private investment to create new housing units as part of the 550 King Street project.

The project will result in approximately 20,000 sf of publicly-accessible open space in Winter Hill in a blighted area consisting of an approximately (i) 7,200 sf "Broadway Pocket Plaza", (ii) 7,700 SF "Sewall Pocket Park", and (iii) 4,700 sf "Pedestrian Mews" landscaped passageway. This will leverage private development to yield a total development of approximately $190 million to construct 288 units of rental housing that includes 132 units at 30% - 80% AMI and 14,000 sf of retail space.

Worcester will make public infrastructure improvements (roadway, streetscape, and water/sewer) to support approximately five housing developments adding 580 new housing units within Worcester's 3A district.
Ayer will create Schematic Designs, Preliminary Engineering and Cost-Estimating for the proposed Ayer Center for Active Living.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: To hire engineering firm to conduct engineering analysis and design for improvements of Milford Street (Route 109) from intersection with Summer Street (Route 126) to Milford Town line, approximately 1.8 miles. Will include surveying, traffic operation and safety analysis of four intersections (West St, Fisher St, Clark St. and Trotter Drive) and evaluation for potential signalization, stormwater management, and street design.

The Town is seeking funds to fully design, engineer, and permit what are now conceptual plans for streetscape improvements in its downtown, from the intersection of Main and Sutton Street, to the intersection of Water and High Street (including some areas of side streets off of Main Street). Improvements to be designed include, but are not limited to, repaving, wider sidewalks, bumpouts, crosswalks with pedestrian signals, ornamental lighting, enhanced landscaping, accomodations for bicyclists.

Salem will conduct an alternatives analysis and prepare a 30% design plan for improvements to the Franklin St corridor in support of 230+ housing units (35% affordable) and ~48,255 sf of commercial space planned near its MBTA Station.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: To hire engineering firm to conduct engineering analysis and design for improvements of Milford Street (Route 109) from intersection with Summer Street (Route 126) to Milford Town line, approximately 1.8 miles. Will include surveying, traffic operation and safety analysis of four intersections and evaluation for potential signalization and stormwater management.

Easton will pursue 75% preconstruction design work for about 1 mile of Foundry St, adding traffic management infrastructure, bicycle lanes, and sidewalks and crosswalks to serve the Furnace Village growth district.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: Framingham will make intersection improvements at Fountain St./Dudley Rd., providing key upgrades along this major gateway into downtown and Cushing Park. Permanent traffic signals will be installed, ADA-accessible sidewalks/crosswalks improved, and dedicated turn lanes.
New Bedford will reconstruct the main north-south corridors of multiple streets with improved pedestrian walkways, signaling, and bicycle accommodation.
Quincy will make improvements to key intersections of Washington at Elm Street.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: Shrewsbury will design a new connector road from South St to Green St near Route 20 that will support the Route 20 widening and a proposed 300-unit 40B development at 33-69 Green St.
data-source: Housing Choice Grant Program Awards | Mass.gov

project: This project will extend Green Street as a Complete Street between Main Street (Route 117) and Weston Street (US Route 20). Grant funds will support construction of 0.9 miles of roadway and streetscape improvements, reliving traffic congestion.

Waltham will extend Green Street as a complete street between Main St. (Rte. 117) and Weston St. (US Rte. 20), constructing roadway, making stormwater, streetscape, and utility improvements, along with intersection and signal modifications, to catalyze the development of up to 1,500 housing units.
Watertown will construct a critical section of its community path along Arsenal Street to support a complete streets project within its 3A district.

Weymouth will conduct the first phase of infrastructure improvements in Jackson Square to support redevelopment, including installing a roundabout, streetscape improvements, and upgrades to water and sewer infrastructure.
This infrastructure project includes installation of 4,397 linear feet of sewer and manhole structures to enable new housing units and retail spaces in a ransit-connected area.
Leominster will upgrade sewer/water/sidewalks in the Marcello Avenue area of North Leominster to help facilitate two affordable housing projects, both on Marcello Avenue for a total of 57 units.
The Littleton Sewer Project will provide critical sewer infrastructure to support the redevelopment of the 550 King Street parcel, unlocking the ability to create a thriving mixed-use development.

The project will replace a 100-year-old concrete box drainage culvert to support the adjacent 78 Crafts Street development project, which aims to provide a significant increase in the neighborhood’s housing opportunities.

The Lafayette Road Sewer project will bring sewer to 100s of undeveloped Commercial/Mixed Use and Residential Acres in Salisbury. It is a priority project and was approved at Town meeting to move forward with construction. The Cost of the project is hindering redevelopment. We are applying for funds to help reduce the cost to the end user and incentivize commercial development in this area.
This project will install approximately 6,500 linear feet of sewer main, a new pump station and generator. Expanded sewer service can support the creation of 428 new transit-oriented housing units.
